We know the total student count is ninety six.
Five students moved from Maplewood to Oakwood.
After this move both dorms have equal numbers.

Let us define the original count for Maplewood.
The sum of these two original counts is ninety six.
The difference between them must be ten students.
This is because moving five students equalizes the totals.
If Maplewood had ten more than Oakwood initially.
Then moving five from Maplewood reduces it by five.
And adding five to Oakwood increases it by five.
The gap closes by ten in total after the move.
So Maplewood started with ten more than Oakwood.
We can set up a simple algebraic equation here.
Let X represent the students in Oakwood originally.
Then Maplewood had X plus ten students originally.
The sum of X and X plus ten equals ninety six.
Two times X plus ten equals ninety six.
Subtract ten from both sides of the equation.
Two times X equals eighty six.
Divide eighty six by two to find X.
The result is forty three students in Oakwood.
So the original number was forty three for Oakwood.
The original number for Maplewood was fifty three.

Both dorms now have exactly forty eight residents.
